text.title: php-phpunit-testing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for php-phpunit-testing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Raw status code comparison produces a minimal error message on failure ("Expected: 200, Actual: 422"). assertOk() prints the full response body when it fails, showing the validation errors or exception message that caused the non-200 status.

failure-mode.description: WithoutMiddleware in tests that cover authenticated endpoints.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: WithoutMiddleware disables auth:sanctum, throttle, and policy gates globally for the test class. Tests pass but do not verify that the endpoint is actually protected. A separate test class without WithoutMiddleware must cover the auth failure path.

failure-mode.description: Mocking Eloquent query builders with Mockery to return fake results in feature tests.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Query builder mocks are brittle — they break when any internal Eloquent method call order changes. They also produce false-positive tests: the mock returns the expected data regardless of whether the actual query is correct. Use RefreshDatabase + factories for feature tests.

failure-mode.description: assertJson([ 'data' => [ 'name' => 'John' ] ]) as the sole response assertion.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: assertJson performs a partial match — it passes even if the response contains extra unexpected fields or nested objects. Use assertJsonPath for specific field assertions and assertJsonMissing for fields that must not appear (e.g., 'password').

failure-mode.description: Time-dependent tests that call now()->addDay() in production code without freezing time in the test with Carbon::setTestNow().
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Tests that depend on wall-clock time are non-deterministic. A test asserting that a token expires at "tomorrow midnight" passes at 10am and fails at 11:59pm UTC if the CI job spans midnight. Always freeze time at the start of the test.

failure-mode.description: actingAs($user) without a separate test for the non-owner getting 403.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A test that only acts as the owner verifies the happy path. It does not verify that the policy blocks the wrong user. Without the 403 test, agents can remove or break the policy gate without any test failing.

failure-mode.description: Feature tests that assert only on the HTTP response status code and not on the DB or the dispatched events.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A controller that returns 201 Created without actually calling the service (due to a DI misconfiguration or early return bug) passes the status assertion. assertDatabaseHas() and Queue::assertPushed() catch this class of bugs.
